title: thymus comptus var. aivalii
intro: Thymus comptus var. aivalii, also known as the Aivalos thyme, is a subspecies of thyme that originates from the Greek island of Lesbos. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with a rich history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Aivalos thyme have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and antiviral effects. Aivalos thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, and to alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
